title: AddrMode.ELIDED case not handled in send()
description:
File: python/src/lichen/link/link_layer.py:208-270, frame.py:36

The send() method accepts addr_mode parameter including ELIDED:

    async def send(
        self,
        payload: bytes,
        dst_addr: bytes = b'',
        addr_mode: AddrMode = AddrMode.NONE,
        ...
    ):

And AddrMode.ELIDED is defined as:
    ELIDED = 3  # derived from IPv6 destination, 0 address bytes

But send() doesn't validate that dst_addr matches addr_mode. If caller passes:
    send(payload, dst_addr=b'\x12\x34', addr_mode=AddrMode.ELIDED)

The LichenFrame._validate() will fail because:
- ELIDED requires 0 address bytes (per _ADDR_LEN_TABLE)
- But dst_addr has 2 bytes

The error happens at frame.to_bytes() call, which is late. Should validate early or document requirements.

Also: For ELIDED mode, where does the address come from? The comment says 'derived from IPv6 destination' but there's no IPv6 context passed to send().
close_reason: Early dst_addr length validation for all AddrMode including ELIDED already present
